input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan for .NET MAUI on Windows 11 (Android + Windows targets):

---

## 1. Install .NET SDK

```powershell
# Check if .NET is already installed
dotnet --list-sdks

# Install via winget (discovers latest stable automatically)
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9 --accept-source-agreements --accept-package-agreements

# Verify
dotnet --version
```

## 2. Install the MAUI Workload

```powershell
# List available workloads to find exact MAUI name
dotnet workload search maui

# Install MAUI (pulls in Android, Windows dependencies)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

## 3. Install Visual Studio Build Tools (Windows target needs WinUI/WinAppSDK)

```powershell
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community --accept-source-agreements --accept-package-agreements

# Then add required components via the VS Installer CLI:
& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installer.exe" modify `
  --installPath "C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\Community" `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.ManagedDesktop `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.Maui.All `
  --passive --norestart
```

## 4. Android SDK Setup

The MAUI workload installs a default Android SDK. To discover and manage it:

```powershell
# Find where the Android SDK was installed
$androidSdk = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk"
if (!(Test-Path $androidSdk)) {
    # Alternative path used by VS
    $androidSdk = "$env:ProgramFiles(x86)\Android\android-sdk"
}
Write-Host "Android SDK at: $androidSdk"

# Discover installed and available platforms/build-tools
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list

# Install required components (discover latest API dynamically)
$available = & "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list 2>&1
$latestPlatform = ($available | Select-String "platforms;android-(\d+)" |
    ForEach-Object { [int]$_.Matches.Groups[1].Value } | Sort-Object -Descending)[0]

Write-Host "Latest platform: android-$latestPlatform"

& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
    "platforms;android-$latestPlatform" `
    "build-tools;$latestPlatform.0.0" `
    "platform-tools"

# Accept all licenses
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --licenses
```

## 5. Set Environment Variables

```powershell
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME", $androidSdk, "User")
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("JAVA_HOME",
    (Get-ChildItem "$env:ProgramFiles\Microsoft\jdk-*" |
     Sort-Object Name -Descending | Select-Object -First 1).FullName, "User")

# Refresh current session
$env:ANDROID_HOME = [System.Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME", "User")
$env:JAVA_HOME    = [System.Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable("JAVA_HOME", "User")
```

## 6. Enable Developer Mode (required for Windows target)

```powershell
# Requires admin
Start-Process powershell -Verb RunAs -ArgumentList @(
    '-Command',
    'reg add "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" /t REG_DWORD /f /v AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense /d 1'
)
```

## 7. Create and Verify a Project

```powershell
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Build for Windows (WinUI)
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Run on Windows
dotnet run -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Run on Android emulator (after creating one via AVD)
dotnet build -f net9.0-android -t:Run
```

## 8. (Optional) Android Emulator

```powershell
# Discover and install a system image
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
    "system-images;android-$latestPlatform;google_apis;x86_64" "emulator"

# Create AVD
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat" create avd `
    -n "Pixel_MAUI" -k "system-images;android-$latestPlatform;google_apis;x86_64" `
    --device "pixel_6"

# Launch
Start-Process "$androidSdk\emulator\emulator.exe" -ArgumentList "-avd Pixel_MAUI"
```

---

**Key notes:**
- Adjust `net9.0` to your SDK version (`dotnet --version` tells you).
- The VS Community install in step 3 is the most reliable way to get WinAppSDK/WinUI bits. If you already have VS, just modify it.
- Run step 6 once (admin required) or toggle it in **Settings → Developer settings**.
data.reasoningText: The user wants a complete step-by-step plan for setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11, targeting Android and Windows. They can't run commands now, so they want all the PowerShell commands laid out. Let me provide a comprehensive guide.
